# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

You are in a training scenario exploring `jj` command options for templates and metadata fields.
A `jj` repository is located at `/home/user/repo` with two existing commits (a base commit and a working copy commit).

Your task is to:
1. Modify the author of the current working copy commit to be exactly `Trainer <trainer@example.com>`.
2. Add a template alias named `training_log` to your global `jj` configuration (`~/.jjconfig.toml`). The template must produce a string containing the short commit ID, the author's email, and the first line of the description, separated by ` - ` (space, hyphen, space), followed by a newline.
3. Generate a log of the current commit and its immediate parent (i.e., the revisions `@` and `@-`) using your new `training_log` template. Save this output to `/home/user/repo/formatted_log.txt`.
I'll break this down into three tasks and execute them systematically.

todos.content: Modify the author of the current working copy commit to Trainer <trainer@example.com>
Add template alias 'training_log' to global jj config (~/.jjconfig.toml)
Generate log of @ and @- using training_log template, save to formatted_log.txt

parts.text: Let me first explore the existing repo and jj configuration to understand the current state.

output.output:
@  rwsuwmys default@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:56 b098f8ad
│  (empty) WIP commit
○  ltkpwmnq default@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:56 55bd2634
│  (empty) Base commit
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

parts.text: I can see the repo has two commits plus the root. The working copy is `rwsuwmys` with description "WIP commit" and its parent is `ltkpwmnq` with "Base commit". No global `~/.jjconfig.toml` exists yet. Let me proceed with all three tasks.

todos.content: Modify the author of the current working copy commit to Trainer <trainer@example.com>
Add template alias 'training_log' to global jj config (~/.jjconfig.toml)
Generate log of @ and @- using training_log template, save to formatted_log.txt

parts.text: **Step 1:** Modify the author of the working copy commit.

output.output:
Warning: `jj describe --no-edit` is deprecated; use `jj metaedit` instead
Warning: `jj describe --author` is deprecated; use `jj metaedit --author` instead
Working copy  (@) now at: rwsuwmys 51fb4ad8 (empty) WIP commit
Parent commit (@-)      : ltkpwmnq 55bd2634 (empty) Base commit

parts.text: The deprecation warnings suggest using `jj metaedit` instead. Let me verify the author was set correctly:
Author is set correctly. Now **Step 2:** Create the global jj config with the template alias.

todos.content: Modify the author of the current working copy commit to Trainer <trainer@example.com>
Add template alias 'training_log' to global jj config (~/.jjconfig.toml)
Generate log of @ and @- using training_log template, save to formatted_log.txt
Modify the author of the current working copy commit to Trainer <trainer@example.com>
Add template alias 'training_log' to global jj config (~/.jjconfig.toml)
Generate log of @ and @- using training_log template, save to formatted_log.txt

parts.text: **Step 3:** Generate the log using the template and save it.

output.content:
44d3881b7ff3 - trainer@example.com - WIP commit
55bd2634b622 - default@example.com - Base commit

parts.text: Let me verify the output looks correct. The working copy now has a different commit ID (because `jj describe` rewrote it). Let me check:

output.output:
@  rwsuwmys trainer@example.com 2026-03-12 04:00:51 d1431cb1
│  WIP commit
○  ltkpwmnq default@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:56 55bd2634
│  (empty) Base commit
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

parts.text: The working copy was modified by the file write, so the commit ID changed. Let me regenerate the log to capture the current state:

output.content:
a0924d17d7d0 - trainer@example.com - WIP commit
55bd2634b622 - default@example.com - Base commit
